notes: curve re-steepened after inversion (+0.74) — historically the riskier phase

allocation.why: Recovery / Goldilocks; recession risk MODERATE; liquidity +33bn/13w
HY OAS 2.69% — no cushion at these spreads

summary.quote_delay_note: Yahoo end-of-day/intraday quotes are delayed up to ~15-20 min depending on exchange; FRED series update on official release schedules.

conclusions.title: Cycle regime: Recovery / Goldilocks
conclusions.detail: Growth rising (payrolls 3m ann +0.8%, IP YoY +1.1%, WEI 2.6) with core inflation momentum below trend (core CPI 3m ann 2.3% vs YoY 2.8%). Favor: Equities (cyclicals, small caps), credit. Avoid: Cash, defensives.

conclusions.title: Net USD liquidity roughly flat (+33bn over 13 weeks)
conclusions.detail: Fed B/S $6.74tn, RRP $0bn (drained), TGA $756bn. Net liquidity $5,987bn. M2 YoY +5.6%. Liquidity direction tends to lead risk assets by weeks.

conclusions.title: Policy is accommodative (FFR 3.63% vs Taylor 4.87%)
conclusions.detail: 2Y at 4.21% prices +58bp of tightening over the next cycle. 10Y real yield 2.35%, breakevens 2.25% (5y5y 2.22%) — inflation expectations anchored.

conclusions.title: Recession risk: MODERATE (1 pts)
conclusions.detail: curve re-steepened after inversion (+0.74) — historically the riskier phase

conclusions.title: Copper/gold +27.2% (3m) — growth impulse improving
conclusions.detail: 6m +26.7%. Copper/gold tracks global IP and bond yields.

conclusions.title: US ISM: manufacturing 53.3, services 54.0 — both expanding
conclusions.detail: PMI above 50 = expansion. Manufacturing back above 50 alongside the copper/gold impulse confirms the goods-cycle upturn.

conclusions.title: India macro: GDP 7.8% YoY, CPI 4.38%, IIP 5.1%
conclusions.detail: CPI inside RBI's 2-6% band; growth strong. WPI at 9.9% is running 5.5pp above CPI — a wholesale-price surge that either squeezes corporate margins or passes through to CPI later; watch pricing-power sectors.

conclusions.title: Country allocation (vs MSCI ACWI): overweight Singapore, Italy, Spain, Poland, Switzerland
conclusions.detail: Improving (accumulate): France, Germany, Australia, India (MSCI). Lagging (avoid/underweight): South Korea. Based on RRG position of iShares MSCI country ETFs vs ACWI.

conclusions.title: US sector leadership: Health Care, Energy, Financials, Real Estate
conclusions.detail: Improving (early rotation candidates): Consumer Staples, Utilities, Communication Services. Lagging (avoid): Technology, Materials, Consumer Discretionary.

conclusions.title: India sector leadership: Nifty Realty, Nifty Media, Nifty Auto, Nifty PSU Bank
conclusions.detail: Improving (early rotation candidates): Nifty PSE. Lagging (avoid): Nifty Energy, Nifty Infra, Nifty FMCG.

conclusions.title: Theme leadership: Regional Banks
conclusions.detail: Improving (early rotation candidates): Oil Services, Copper Miners, Gold Miners. Lagging (avoid): Semiconductors, Software, US Infrastructure.
